mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined by the unemployment rate rising more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window, signals imminent recession risk that triggers downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the hazard ratio for negative returns does not exceed 1.5 relative to non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated monthly inflation and high policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that simultaneously erodes real consumer purchasing power and increases corporate borrowing costs, leading to sustained negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression of 60-day returns on CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a coefficient with p-value > 0.10, or forward returns are positive in over 60% of high-inflation/high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery regimes, month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvements, which directly boost cyclical sector earnings and generate positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no predictive power for 5-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of positive INDPRO growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth serves as the primary driver of aggregate corporate revenue expansion; positive GDP acceleration consistently translates into upward earnings revisions that push the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward returns exhibit zero correlation (Pearson r < 0.05), or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in at least two consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index registers a month-over-month increase exceeding 0.8 percent, markets price in aggressive Federal Reserve intervention, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly prints above the threshold do not precede negative 20-day returns more than 45% of the time, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate signals structural labor market frictions that precede corporate margin compression and equity volatility, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The cross-product term of PAYEMS and UNRATE changes fails to significantly predict 20-day equity returns (p > 0.05), or the signal generates positive forward returns in more than 55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In housing market expansion regimes, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and durable goods spending, providing short-term positive momentum for the S&P 500 over a 5-day trading horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST monthly changes show no statistically significant relationship with 5-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5), or the signal underperforms the market median in more than 60% of positive housing start months.
